perturbation.original_text: 4.1 At the request of the supplier of the Proprietary Information the receiving party shall immediately return all Proprietary Information which the receiving party (or an Authorised Person/Agency Authorised Person) has in its possession, custody or control including all materials (in hardcopy or electronic form) that incorporate any part of the Proprietary Information and, if required, destroy any copies of such Proprietary Information and materials and certify in writing that such destruction has been completed.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 At the request of the supplier of the Proprietary Information the receiving party *may, at their discretion,* return all Proprietary Information which the receiving party (or an Authorised Person/Agency Authorised Person) has in its possession, custody or control including all materials (in hardcopy or electronic form) that incorporate any part of the Proprietary Information and, if required, destroy any copies of such Proprietary Information and materials and certify in writing that such destruction has been completed.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states a clear obligation: the receiving party *shall* immediately return the Proprietary Information upon request. The inserted phrase 'may, at their discretion' transforms this obligation into an option. This is contradictory and creates uncertainty. The original obligation is now unenforceable due to the added discretion. This affects the assurances made about the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: At the request of the supplier of the Proprietary Information the receiving party may, at their discretion, return all Proprietary Information which the receiving party (or an Authorised Person/Agency Authorised Person) has in its possession, custody or control including all materials (in hardcopy or electronic form) that incorporate any part of the Proprietary Information and, if required, destroy any copies of such Proprietary Information and materials and certify in writing that such destruction has been completed.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 4.1 uses 'shall' indicating a mandatory action for the receiving party to return Proprietary Information. The changed text replaces 'shall' with 'may, at their discretion,' giving the receiving party the option, not the obligation, to return the information. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text which makes it a contradiction. This directly contradicts the initial mandatory requirement and creates a strong in-text contradiction because the obligation to return the information upon request is now optional, thus weakening the protection of the Proprietary Information and violating the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Termination 6.1 The Agency may terminate this agreement at any time by notice in writing if; a) the Contractor becomes insolvent, ceases to carry out its business, has a receiver, liquidator, administrative r eceiver, administrator, trustee or other similar officer appointed over the whole or part of its assets or an order is made or a resolution passed for the winding up of the Contractor (save for a solvent winding up as part of a bona fide reconstruction or amalgamation); or b) the Contractor commits a breach of this agreement or of the Contract which is material and not capable of remedy or which is capable of remedy but which is not remedied within 60 days of notice to the Agency.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Termination 6.1 The Agency may terminate this agreement *only* if the Contractor becomes insolvent, ceases to carry out its business, has a receiver, liquidator, administrative r eceiver, administrator, trustee or other similar officer appointed over the whole or part of its assets or an order is made or a resolution passed for the winding up of the Contractor (save for a solvent winding up as part of a bona fide reconstruction or amalgamation); or b) the Contractor commits a breach of this agreement or of the Contract which is material and not capable of remedy or which is capable of remedy but which is not remedied within 60 days of notice to the Agency. 6.2 The Contractor may termi nate this agreement if the Contract is cancelled by the Agency for some reason other than the fault of the Contractor. 6.3 *However, the agency cannot terminate the agreement*
perturbation.explanation: Section 6.1 originally allows the Agency to terminate the agreement 'at any time' under specific conditions (insolvency or breach). By adding 'only', the Agency's termination rights are restricted to only those specific conditions. Then, adding 'However, the agency cannot terminate the agreement' contradicts both the 'at any time' and the specified conditions, and giving rise to the uncertainty of whether the agency can really terminate the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: However, the agency cannot terminate the agreement
perturbation.justification: The original text allows the Agency to terminate the agreement 'at any time' under specific conditions. The changed text adds 'only', restricting the Agency's termination rights to those specific conditions. Then, the added clause 'However, the agency cannot terminate the agreement' directly contradicts both the 'at any time' clause and the specified conditions in the changed text itself. The 'Contradicted Text' is exactly the contradictory phrase added to the Change Text, which creates a strong in-text contradiction. This fits the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition, as the placement of the contradicting clause directly creates uncertainty about the agency's rights to terminate the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 7.3 For the avoidance of doubt, all obligations in this agreement concerning the access, use, distribution, circulation and copying of Proprietary Information supplied by the Agency and the Contractor remain valid and enforceab le following completion of the Contract.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.3 For the avoidance of doubt, all obligations in this agreement concerning the access, use, distribution, circulation and copying of Proprietary Information supplied by the Agency and the Contractor *cease* following completion of the Contract.
perturbation.explanation: This clause originally states clearly that the obligations regarding Proprietary Information *remain* valid even after the contract is completed. Changing 'remain' to 'cease' creates a direct contradiction. This impacts the long-term protection of sensitive information, as it is now unclear whether confidentiality obligations continue post-contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the avoidance of doubt, all obligations in this agreement concerning the access, use, distribution, circulation and copying of Proprietary Information supplied by the Agency and the Contractor cease following completion of the Contract.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that obligations concerning Proprietary Information remain valid after the contract's completion. The changed text explicitly states that these obligations cease after completion. The contradicted text agrees with the Changed text, stating obligations cease, and so it contradicts the original. This is a direct, clear, and impactful contradiction regarding the enforceability of confidentiality post-contract, fulfilling the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition because the same clause is presented in multiple locations with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty about the obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Appendix 1 Authorised Persons The following individuals named by the Contractor have read and understood the terms and conditions of this Non -Disclosure Agreement dated [ ] and their signature confirms agreement to be bound by its terms.
perturbation.changed_text: Appendix 1 Authorised Persons The following individuals named by the Contractor *may or may not* have read and understood the terms and conditions of this Non -Disclosure Agreement dated [ ] and their signature confirms agreement to be bound by its terms.
perturbation.explanation: The original text assumes and states that individuals listed in Appendix 1 have understood the agreement. Adding 'may or may not' introduces uncertainty about their understanding, even though their signature confirms their agreement. This casts doubt on the enforceability of the agreement against these individuals, as it can be argued that they didn't fully comprehend the terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Authorised Persons The following individuals named by the Contractor may or may not have read and understood the terms and conditions of this Non -Disclosure Agreement dated [ ] and their signature confirms agreement to be bound by its terms.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces the phrase 'may or may not' regarding the individuals' understanding of the agreement, while their signature confirms their agreement to be bound by its terms. This creates a contradiction, as the confirmation of agreement implies understanding, which is directly contradicted by the uncertainty introduced by 'may or may not'. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text, indicating that the introduction of 'may or may not' is the source of the contradiction. According to the explanation, this introduces uncertainty about their understanding, even though their signature confirms their agreement. This contradicts the idea that signing implies understanding and agreement to all terms, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This fits under Structural Flaws definition as the clause is in multiple locations, with the changed text causing conflicting implications.
